channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Philippine ex-President Duterte set to appear in Hague courtroom to face 'war on drugs' charges
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — Former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte is scheduled to make his first appearance before judges of the International Criminal Court on Friday, days after his stunning arrest in Manila on murder charges linked to the deadly “ war on drugs ” he oversaw while in office
item.dc:creator: MIKE CORDER, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: China, Russia and Iran call for end to US sanctions on Iran and the restart of nuclear talks
item.description: Representatives of China, Russia and Iran called for an end to U.S. sanctions on Iran over its rapidly advancing nuclear program and a restart to multinational talks on the issue.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Bodeen, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Europe's military personnel shortfalls exposed as Trump warns US security priorities lie elsewhere
item.description: The Trump administration’s warning last month that Europe must now take care of security in its own backyard raises troubling questions.
item.dc:creator: Lorne Cook, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Pope receives cake and glad tidings on the 12th anniversary of his papacy, marked in the hospital
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis marked the 12th anniversary of his pontificate Thursday in the hospital — the first pope to do so in modern church history — as he received increasingly positive medical news on his recovery, a cake and hundreds of messages wishing him well.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Guatemala steps up patrols along border as US extends border security goals south
item.description: OCOS, Guatemala (AP) — As the United States government works to effectively extend its border security objectives south into Central America, countries like Guatemala have come under renewed scrutiny and pressure to step up their own border enforcement.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UN report accuses Israel of sexual violence against Palestinians. Netanyahu claims anti-Israel bias
item.description: GENEVA (AP) — United Nations-backed human rights experts on Thursday accused Israel of “the systematic use of sexual, reproductive and other gender-based violence” in its war with Hamas in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Mexicans searching for missing relatives uncover possible mass killing site in Jalisco state
item.description: TEUCHITLAN, Mexico (AP) — When a group of citizens searching for missing relatives in the western state of Jalisco arrived at a remote ranch outside Mexico's second-largest city last week on an anonymous tip, all they had to do was push open the unlocked gate.
item.dc:creator: DIANA DE LA MORA and FABIOLA SÁNCHEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ontario Premier Ford hails 'positive' meeting with Lutnick
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford lauded his Thursday meeting with Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick as “positive” and “productive” after their public rift over tariffs on imported goods.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Putin agrees in principle with proposal for Ukraine ceasefire and says more discussions are needed
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin said Thursday that he agrees in principle with a U.S. proposal for a 30-day ceasefire in Ukraine, but he emphasized that the terms are yet to be worked out and added that any truce should pave the way to lasting peace.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Poll: Majority of Americans think Trump is too close to Russia
item.description: A majority of Americans think President Trump is too closely aligned with Russia, including more than a quarter of Republicans, a poll released Thursday found.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Sales of US goods 'rapidly dropping' at Canadian grocery stores
item.description: Canadians are buying fewer products made in the U.S. in an effort to brace for the impact of President Trump’s sweeping tariffs, which are set to cause prices to skyrocket across a range of products.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: UN official denies seeing 'a shred of evidence' showing staff in Gaza held hostages
item.description: Tom Fletcher, a top UN humanitarian aid official, says he has not seen "a shred of evidence" linking the international body's workers in Gaza to Hamas hostages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump signals cautious optimism on Russia accepting Ukraine ceasefire
item.description: President Trump on Thursday said he hoped Russia would “do the right thing” as his administration presses for a ceasefire agreement between Moscow and Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Trump pushes annexation of Greenland during meeting with NATO secretary
item.description: President Trump on Thursday expressed confidence the United States would annex Greenland, even suggesting the head of the NATO alliance could be a key player in facilitating the acquisition.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Putin thanks Trump for principles of ceasefire push, but does not say yes
item.description: Russian President Putin says he agrees in "principle" with President Trump's desire for a 30-day ceasefire but argued it would only help Ukraine and said he wants a lasting peace plan.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pakistan's leader meets survivors of deadly train hijacking and the commandos who ended the siege
item.description: QUETTA, Pakistan (AP) — Pakistan’s prime minister on Thursday commended the country’s armed forces for successfully rescuing 339 passengers after a deadly train hijacking by insurgents in the southwest. A total of 25 people were killed by the attackers.
item.dc:creator: ABDUL SATTAR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration empties Guantánamo of migrants, flies all back to US
item.description: The Trump administration’s plans to detain migrants in Guantánamo Bay, Cuba, hit another roadblock this week after all 40 such individuals held there were sent back to the United States, with no future deportation flights scheduled to the island.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration resumes immigrant family detentions
item.description: The Trump administration has resumed family detention of immigrants, a legal nonprofit said, reigniting a controversial practice largely curtailed under the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump threatens 200% tariff on European wine after proposed whiskey tariff
item.description: Trump vowed a new escalation in his trade war if the EU pushes ahead with the planned 50% tariff on American whiskey.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The Asian elephant population in Cambodia is more robust than previously thought, a study finds
item.description: A genetic study of Asian elephants in northern Cambodia reveals a larger and more robust population than previously thought, raising hopes the endangered species could slowly recover.
item.dc:creator: Sopheng Cheang And David Rising, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ethiopia's Tigray regional government appeals for help after factional fighters seize key towns
item.description: ADDIS ABABA, Ethiopia (AP) — The interim government of Ethiopia’s Tigray region appealed for the Ethiopian federal government to intervene after a faction of the Tigray People's Liberation Front seized control of two major towns, leaving several people wounded and raising fears of a return to civil
item.dc:creator: SAMUEL GETACHEW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel says Hamas sent a toddler to a military outpost
item.description: Israeli soldiers spotted a 4-year-old child approaching their military outpost, who was apparently sent by Hamas. Israel says the boy was returned to Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Argentine retirees and soccer fans clash with police during protests against austerity measures
item.description: BUENOS AIRES, Argentina (AP) — Argentine retirees and fans of several soccer teams clashed with police Wednesday during a protest in front of Congress in the capital, Buenos Aires, against the economic policies implemented by the government of President Javier Milei.
item.dc:creator: SARA ESPAÑA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UN launches reform initiative as it nears its 80th anniversary and faces funding cuts
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The U.N. chief launched a new initiative Wednesday to reform the United Nations as it approaches its 80th anniversary, saying the 193-member global organization needs an urgent update to deal with major funding reductions and still tackle the world's challenges.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says tariffs will be 'positive'; Vance pushes Russia for ceasefire
item.description: “The United States of America is going to take back a lot of what was stolen from it by other countries.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Pakistani insurgents attack a train carrying hundreds of people and take hostages
item.description: QUETTA, Pakistan (AP) — Pakistani insurgents attacked a passenger train carrying several hundred people as it passed through a tunnel on Tuesday and claimed to have taken more than 100 hostages, though officials later said at least 104 were rescued.
item.dc:creator: ABDUL SATTAR, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US resumes military aid and intelligence sharing as Ukraine says it is open to a 30-day ceasefire
item.description: JEDDAH, Saudi Arabia (AP) — The Trump administration lifted its suspension of military aid and intelligence sharing for Ukraine, and Kyiv signaled that it was open to a 30-day ceasefire in the war with Russia, pending Moscow’s agreement, American and Ukrainian officials said Tuesday following talks
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: They were forced to scam others worldwide; now thousands are detained on the Burmese border
item.description: Survivors have found themselves in crowded facilities without medical care; 7,000 people were freed last month after being trapped in locked compounds in Burma where they were forced to scam people.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: British tourist detained in ICE center after visa suspension: Family
item.description: In a statement to NewsNation, an ICE spokesperson said Burke was detained by the agency "for violation of the terms and conditions of her admission.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Shafer, NewsNation

item.title: See it: Activists vandalize Trump's Scotland golf resort
item.description: Pro-Palestinian activists said Saturday they vandalized one of Trump's golf resorts in Scotland in response to his proposal to empty the Gaza Strip of its Palestinian population.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: In his own words: Pope Francis' views on resigning changed over time
item.description: ROME (AP) — As he recovers from pneumonia in the hospital, Pope Francis has increasingly handed off his day-to-day responsibilities to cardinals as questions swirl about the near and long-term future of his papacy.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Unexploded WWII-era bomb causes travel chaos in Paris, disrupts Europe's busiest railway hub
item.description: The discovery of a huge unexploded World War II-era bomb caused transportation chaos in Paris on Friday, including the suspension of high-speed train links with London and Brussels and the closure of a vital road artery into the French capital.
item.dc:creator: Samuel Petrequin, WGN 9
